title: Adreanna Escalante
role_summary: Nominator of [[people/marcos-garibay]] for the 2023 CRFW Fund cycle.
body:
# Adreanna Escalante

Nominated **Marcos Garibay** for the 2023 CRFW Fund grant cycle. Per the August 2023 email summary, RedLine was "getting a link from her that was missing in the application regarding Marcos's work and some more info on the school/community center, etc that he might work with" — implying she has a community-arts-program connection in the Denver area, plausibly tied to the **Adams 14 / Commerce City school district** where Garibay worked at Dupont Elementary.

## Research gaps

- Own role/practice/affiliation not yet identified.
- Whether she has a continuing relationship with the fund (other nominations, jury work) is unknown.

## Web-search status (2026-04-25 → 2026-04-26)

**Session 13 (2026-04-25):** Public-web sweep on `"Adreanna Escalante"
Denver arts` returned no match. Spelling variants (Adriana, Adrianna) not
yet tried; flagged as a small follow-up.

**Session 14 (2026-04-26):** Spelling variants tried and exhausted.
`"Adriana Escalante" Denver arts Colorado` surfaces only an unrelated
medical-liaison at Ramos Law and a separate Adriana Gonzales who is a
Denver-area actress (different surname). `"Adrianna Escalante" Denver
arts` returns Adrianna Abarca (Latino Cultural Arts Center founder,
different surname) and unrelated tourism-foundation pages. **Public-web is
fully exhausted** across the original spelling and both common variants.

Path forward is **Gmail**: thread metadata may include her email-domain
and/or signature with affiliation, and a name-and-domain search across
other CRFW threads could surface other interactions she's had with the
fund. Likely vehicle: a brief Gmail-MCP sweep narrowed to the August 2023
grant-cycle window.

## Source

- Gmail thread `189f5e407e08005a` — "Fwd: CRFW Fund - Grant Cycle" (August 2023)
